title: Berlin by Foot - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin by foot works best in chosen areas, not across the whole city.** Walk inside one strong zone, then use public transport between far-apart districts.
**For a first morning, choose Museum Island, Unter den Linden and Brandenburg Gate.** It gives the most history and orientation per step.
**Tiergarten is the best breathing-room walk** when the centre feels intense or your group needs shade, benches and a calmer rhythm.
**Kreuzberg, East Side Gallery and Prenzlauer Berg are better second-layer walks.** They are excellent after the classic sights, but do not force them into the same long route.
**Use the 25 to 30 minute rule.** If the next interesting place is more than half an hour of dull walking away, ride and save your feet for the walk that matters.
